title: Dòkò Numbaz!
description: A fun calypso song teaching numbers 1-10 in Dominican style with a touch of Kwéyòl.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
One sun, shining bright [CLAP]
Making everything feel right
Two mangoes, sweet and gold [SNAP]
A story to be told

[Chorus]
Dòkò numbaz, let's all count!
From one to ten, a big amount!
Dòkò numbaz, sing along!
With a happy, joyful song!

[Verse 2]
Three hummingbirds, wings so fast [STOMP]
Memories that forever last
Four crabs, on the sandy shore [CLAP]
Wanting to find some more

[Chorus]
Dòkò numbaz, let's all count!
From one to ten, a big amount!
Dòkò numbaz, sing along!
With a happy, joyful song!

[Verse 3]
Five flowers, petals bright [SNAP]
Dancing in the morning light
Six coconuts, tall and green [STOMP]
The best sight I've ever seen

[Verse 4]
Seven stars way up high,
Twinkling in the Dominican sky.
Eight goats, they like to play,
In the fields all through the day.

[Chorus]
Dòkò numbaz, let's all count!
From one to ten, a big amount!
Dòkò numbaz, sing along!
With a happy, joyful song!

[Outro]
Nine children, hand in hand,
Exploring our beautiful land.
Ten fingers, waving free,
'Mèsi anpil'* for counting with me!

*Mèsi anpil: Thank you very much (Kwéyòl)
thumbnailPrompt: A vibrant and colorful illustration depicting a sunny Dominican landscape with lush green mountains and a sparkling blue sea. In the foreground, a group of diverse children are dancing and playing musical instruments like steelpan, drums, and shakers. The numbers 1 through 10 are playfully integrated into the scenery, appearing on mangoes, flowers, coconuts, and even as stars in the sky. The overall style should be cheerful and inviting, capturing the spirit of Caribbean music and culture. Style: Children's book illustration.
educationalValue: Teaches children numbers 1-10, introduces Dominican culture, and reinforces counting skills through an engaging song.
